items.title: Rubio, Iran Exchange Signals Rising Tensions as Trump Considers Next Steps
items.desc: As President Donald Trump weighs broader military options against Iran, increasingly pointed exchanges between Washington and Tehran underscore mounting diplomatic strain, while Congress, energy markets, and regional security remain on edge.

items.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Warn Civilians to Stay Away from U.S. Forces Across Middle East
items.desc: "We warn the general public in countries where American soldiers are stationed to immediately stay out of a radius of 500 metres from the places where American soldiers are hiding, in order to be safe," the Guards said in a statement carried by state television.

items.title: Feraşîn Highlands Thrive as Rich Pastures Boost Livestock and Dairy Output
items.desc: Abundant rainfall has revitalized Northern Kurdistan's Feraşîn Highlands' pastures, improving grazing conditions, increasing milk production, and supporting seasonal livestock farming and traditional cheese and yogurt production across the mountain region.

items.title: Coalition Forces Down Five Explosive Drones Over Erbil, CTD Says
items.desc: According to the CTD, the drones were intercepted between 9:32 a.m. and 9:45 a.m. after being launched from the western part of Erbil province. The agency said no casualties or material damage were reported.

items.title: Cowpeas and Figs Put Taq Taq at the Center of Kurdistan's Harvest Season
items.desc: Peak harvest season in Taq Taq is sending 75 tons of cowpeas to markets each day, while the area's renowned figs and expanding agricultural trade are creating jobs and supporting rural incomes across the Kurdistan Region.

items.title: Suicide bombing kills 14 at Pakistan military post
items.desc: Shafaq News- Islamabad A suicide bombing at a military post in Pakistan's northwestern Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province killed at least 14 people and injured 24 others, security sources told Chinese Xinhua news agency on Friday. The explosion destroyed the post and left nine soldiers, four police officers, and one provincial forest department official dead. The wounded included 20 soldiers and four pol

items.title: Dhi Qar conference backs Iraq's anti-corruption campaign
items.desc: Shafaq News- Dhi Qar Community leaders, tribal elders, academics, and representatives of civil society in Iraq's Dhi Qar province held a conference on Friday to express support for the judiciary and Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi's anti-corruption campaign. Shafaq News correspondent reported that participants also backed the federal government's plans to conclude agreements with international compani

items.title: Diyala water project stalls after 10+ years
items.desc: Shafaq News- Diyala A water supply project in Jadidat al-Shatt in Iraq's eastern Diyala province remains unfinished 14 years after construction began, despite an original completion timeline of 390 days, the sub-district administration told Shafaq News on Friday. Awarded to a French company in 2012, the project was initially scheduled for completion within 390 days. Construction stalled because of

items.title: Global Coalition downs five drones over Erbil
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil The Kurdistan Region’s Counter-Terrorism Directorate reported on Friday that Global Coalition air defenses shot down five explosive drones over Erbil. The drones were launched from west of the province, causing no casualties. Falling debris sparked a fire in dry grass and shrubs, which Erbil Civil Defense teams extinguished. items.title: Iraq’s PM al-Zaidi offers Tehran and Washington a corridor, not a battlefield
items.desc: Shafaq News Iraqi Prime Minister Ali Falih al-Zaidi traveled to Tehran on Thursday for a one-day visit that produced railway agreements, a twinning arrangement between the two capitals, and a commitment to draft a comprehensive strategic document. items.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
items.desc: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

items.title: This new bridge aimed to show US-Canada unity but instead stoked divisions
items.desc: A bridge linking Michigan to Ontario is about to open but Canada has disinvited US dignitaries from the ceremony.

items.title: Using a bathroom 20 times a day - life in the middle of US cyclospora outbreak
items.desc: A historic cyclospora outbreak spreading across the US is causing havoc in pockets of Michigan, the hardest hit state.

items.title: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for teen girl's family
items.desc: The court hears the singer ordered a body bag, chainsaws and an inflatable pool to dispose of the teen's body.

items.title: US health panel loosens restrictions for controversial peptides popular online
items.desc: The vote marks a win for Health Secretary Robert F Kennedy who has said he's a big fan of the drugs, which haven't been well studied for human use.

items.title: Oil surges to $100 per barrel. And, Trump imposes a new round of tariffs
items.desc: Oil surged to $100 per barrel after Iran-backed Houthi rebels attacked two Saudi tankers in the Red Sea. And, President Trump overnight replaced expiring tariffs with a slew of new ones.

items.title: Trump threatens a major escalation in Iran as the war rounds its 4th month
items.desc: The U.S. continued airstrikes in Iran while Tehran launched retaliatory attacks on U.S. targets in the Gulf, as President Trump said he was close to deciding whether to launch "a massive attack."

items.title: How the Houthis blockade of the Red Sea could affect the global economy
items.desc: NPR's A Martínez asks former U.S. deputy national security adviser Jon Finer what the blockade of the Red Sea by Houthi rebels means for the global economy.

items.title: US allies hit with new tariffs object to Trump's forced labor claims
items.desc: Australia and the European Union were among U.S. trade partners who condemned the tariffs.

items.title: Netflix viewers the world over are binge-watching South African series 'The Polygamist.' Here's why
items.desc: Why is a Zulu-language drama taking the world by storm? The Polygamist mixes sex and scandal with a sharp portrait of modern South Africa.

items.title: Four Palestinians and one Israeli killed in West Bank shooting
